review_title: Gardio Needed for Chromebook Chrome - CHROME USERS BEWARE BEFORE BUYING THIS PRODUCT
description: I took the time to really evaluate this project as you have read. I recommend you stop selling this product and stop taking advantage of those who don't know best.
author: Jeffrey F.
pros: There is none - just another product trying to get you to spend you money for really not useful information from its scan.
cons: Your assessment of Guardio needed on Chromebook as an extension to protect against malware, information leaks, and attempts to harm your hard drive. This is just absolutely not true for the money you want for this product to only get partial information that makes sense or is really useful to us Chrome users. Before you think I'm steering you wrong, and proving wrong that it's a waste of money, do the following: 1) Google 'what operating system does Chromebooks run" You will see, Chrome OS, which is what Google named their modified Ubuntu Unix operating system. With that proved here is a link about malware and virus penetration: https://www.techadvisor.com/how-to/laptop/can-chromebook-get-virus-3809003/ 2) Install the product and let it do the scan on your Chromebook. I did. I've been using my Chromebook and obviously Chrome for over 5 years. I ran the scan. I found 2 harmful sites, 1 harmful site 3) Most files are stored on the cloud and only a few files are stored on the internal memory of the laptop . 4) Chromebooks run on a modified unix operating system which viruses and malware cannot penetrate. 5) There is no hard drive in a Chromebook - Chromebooks use flash memory to start the operating system and then Chrome. I've now just uninstalled it. Hmmm I wonder if it checked itself for any issues it looks for.
